rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deft Ericsson, Inc'S Further Responses To Written Discovery; And To Compel Deposition Of Deft'S Person Most Qualified Custodian Of Records; Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Ericsson, Inc.¿s further responses to written discovery, to compel deposition of defendant¿s person most qualified and custodian of records, and request for sanctions. No opposition was filed, but a review of LexisNexis File & Serve shows that defendant filed supplemental responses. Because it is not clear whether there are any remaining issues on this motion, if any party believes there is any need for a hearing, no later than 4:30pm today that party should send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain and then both sides must appear at the hearing at 9am on January 27. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Schneider Electric¿s further responses to written discovery, to compel deposition of defendant¿s person most qualified and custodian of records, and request for sanctions is continued on the court¿s own motion to February 8 to give the parties an opportunity to engage in further meet and confer efforts to narrow the scope of the information sought from defendants to a reasonably defined product or products attributable to defendant that plaintiffs in good faith contend that Mr. Gauger was exposed to. If all issues raised by this motion are not resolved before then, by 4:30pm on February 7 each side must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain and then both sides must appear at the hearing at 9am on February 8. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Stanley Ho And For The Production Of Documents And Specific Areas Of Questioning; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of Stanley Ho and for the production of documents and specific areas of questioning is denied. Plaintiffs have not provided any evidence to contradict Rockwell¿s representation (which appears to be supported by Mr. Ho¿s deposition testimony) that there are no responsive documents concerning the second sentence of paragraph 8 in Mr. Ho¿s declaration signed on September 16. Since there are no responsive documents, there is no need for an additional session of Mr. Ho¿s deposition. Both parties¿ requests for sanctions are denied. Plaintiffs¿ position has arguable merit given the wording of the second sentence of paragraph 8 of Mr. Ho¿s declaration. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiffs' Interrogatories By Mecedes Benz Usa, Inc. And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard interrogatories by Mercedes Benz USA, Inc. and request for sanctions will be heard in Dept. 604 at 9:00am, Hon. Marla J. Miller presiding. Plaintiffs¿ motion is denied. Plaintiffs failed to properly meet and confer prior to filing this motion and failed to include a separate statement as required by CRC 3.1345. Defendant¿s request for sanctions against plaintiffs¿ counsel is granted in the amount of $770, pursuant to CCP 2023.010(h) and (i). = (220/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiffs' Interrogatories By Bentley Motors, Inc. And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel responses to General Order 129 Standard Plaintiffs¿ Interrogatories by Bentley Motors, Inc. will be heard in Dept. 604 at 9:00am, Hon. Marla J. Miller presiding. Plaintiffs¿ motion is denied. Plaintiffs failed to properly meet and confer prior to filing this motion and failed to include a separate statement as required by CRC 3.1345. Defendant¿s request for sanctions against plaintiffs¿ counsel is granted in the amount of $1453, pursuant to CCP 2023.010(h) and (i). = (220/MJM)
